title: Theodore Roosevelt wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Theodore Roosevelt, also known as Teddy or T.R., was the 26th president of the United States, serving from 1901 to 1909.

title: Jimmy Carter wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: James Earl Carter Jr. was an American politician and humanitarian who served as the 39th president of the United States from 1977 to 1981.

title: Martin Luther King Jr. wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Martin Luther King Jr. was an American civil rights activist and Baptist minister who was a prominent leader of the civil rights movement from 1955 until his assassination in 1968.

title: Nelson Mandela wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Nelson Rolihlahla Mandela was a South African anti-apartheid activist and statesman who was the first president of South Africa from 1994 to 1999.

title: Mother Teresa wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Mary Teresa Bojaxhiu, better known as Mother Teresa or Saint Mother Teresa, was an Albanian-Indian Catholic nun, founder of the Missionaries of Charity and a Catholic saint.

title: Malala Yousafzai wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Malala Yousafzai is a Pakistani female education activist, and producer of film and television.

title: Mikhail Gorbachev wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Mikhail Sergeyevich Gorbachev was a Soviet and Russian politician who was the last leader of the Soviet Union from 1985 until the country's dissolution in 1991.

title: Henry Kissinger wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Henry Alfred Kissinger was an American diplomat, political scientist, and politician.

title: Woodrow Wilson wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Thomas Woodrow Wilson was the 28th president of the United States, serving from 1913 to 1921.

title: Yasser Arafat wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Yasser Arafat, also popularly known by his kunya Abu Ammar, was a Palestinian political leader.

title: Al Gore wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Albert Arnold Gore Jr. is an American politician, businessman, and environmentalist who served as the 45th vice president of the United States from 1993 to 2001 under President Bill Clinton.

title: Tenzin Gyatso wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: The 14th Dalai Lama is the incumbent Dalai Lama, the highest spiritual leader and head of Tibetan Buddhism.

title: Aung San Suu Kyi wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Aung San Suu Kyi is a Burmese politician, diplomat and author who served as State Counsellor of Myanmar and Minister of Foreign Affairs from 2016 to 2021.

title: Anwar Sadat wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Muhammad Anwar el-Sadat was an Egyptian politician and military officer who served as the 3rd president of Egypt, from 15 October 1970 until his assassination by members of the Egyptian Islamic Jihad on 6 October 1981.

title: Shimon Peres wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Shimon Peres was an Israeli politician and statesman who served as the prime minister of Israel from 1984 to 1986 and from 1995 to 1996 and as the president of Israel from 2007 to 2014.

title: Muhammad Yunus wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Muhammad Yunus is a Bangladeshi economist and social business entrepreneur.

title: Kofi Annan wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Kofi Atta Annan was a Ghanaian diplomat and statesman who served as the seventh secretary-general of the United Nations from 1997 to 2006.

title: Lech Wałęsa wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Lech Wałęsa is a Polish statesman, dissident and Nobel Peace Prize laureate who served as the president of Poland between 1990 and 1995.

title: Dag Hammarskjöld wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Dag Hjalmar Agne Carl Hammarskjöld was a Swedish economist and diplomat who served as the second secretary-general of the United Nations from April 1953 until his death in a plane crash in September 1961.

title: Willy Brandt wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Willy Brandt was a German politician and statesman who was leader of the Social Democratic Party of Germany (SPD) from 1964 to 1987 and concurrently served as the chancellor of West Germany from 1969 to 1974.

title: Albert Schweitzer wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Ludwig Philipp Albert Schweitzer was a German polymath from Alsace.

title: Wangari Muta Maathai wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Wangarĩ Maathai was a Kenyan social, environmental, and political activist who founded the Green Belt Movement, an environmental non-governmental organization focused on planting trees, environmental conservation, and women's rights.

title: Ellen Johnson Sirleaf wins the Nobel Peace Prize
fact: Ellen Eugenia Johnson Sirleaf is a Liberian politician who served as the 24th president of Liberia from 2006 to 2018.
